passages.passage_text: For the purposes of this study, ten qualities of a liveable city were considered including low crime rate, cleanliness, cultural attractions, and other amenities. For each city in the study, scores from 1 (lowest) to 10 (highest) were assigned for each of the ten qualities. The ten scores for each city were then summed, yielding a total liveability score for each city. We hope the resulting ratings will help you in choosing your next place of residence. The passage above makes which of the following assumptions?
An underlying assumption of the passage is that
The argument above makes which of the following assumptions?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Anthropologists have uncovered evidence that human beings, even during paleolithic times, had a belief in life after death. Burial sites throughout eastern and central prehistoric societies, some of which date to the paleolithic era, in which the dead were buried with some of the food, clothing, and implements they might have used in life, support this view. This is direct evidence of a belief in life after death underlying attachment to material things. Which of the following is an assumption underlying this reasoning?

choices.A: The placement of burial sites near ancient settlements indicates a degree of piety towards the dead.
choices.B: Belief in life after death is a central tenet of most religious faiths.
choices.C: The remains of tools and clothing found in the burial sites had belonged to those buried nearby.
choices.D: Religious belief is a characteristic of the most civilised societies.
choices.E: Only those people who believe in life after death would bury useful items with the dead.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Some of the most popular television advertisements are those employing humour. But as an advertising technique, humour has its drawbacks. Some studies have shown that while many viewers of humorous advertisements will readily recall the commercials, far fewer recall the name of the product being promoted. This casts doubt on the ability of humorous commercial advertising to increase product sales.

choices.A: Humorous commercials tend to reduce the credibility of products in the eyes of viewers.
choices.B: Though enjoyable, humorous commercials are often less memorable than serious commercials.
choices.C: The most important criterion of a successful advertisement is its ability to increase product sales.
choices.D: Humorous commercials may alienate almost as many viewers as they entertain.
choices.E: The ultimate goal of advertising is to increase the name recognition of the product being promoted.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Religious controversies were once expected to express and articulate lasting moral values. This is no longer the case. Today, we find spokespeople for major religious denominations entering into debates on mere political matters formerly reserved for members of the secular community. The topical structure of the passage above depends upon the unstated assumption that the expression of lasting moral values is:

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: It is possible to assign an accurate numerical score to each of the city's amenities.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Superficially, today's problems with the abuse of illegal drugs such as heroin and cocaine resemble the abuse of alcohol during the 1920s, when many people kept drinking in spite of prohibition. There is, however, a significant difference. The abuse of drugs such as heroin and cocaine has never been a widespread, socially-accepted practice among most middle-class, otherwise law-abiding Americans. most Americans: A) Using drugs is widely available, they will be abused despite laws to the contrary. B) most Americans consider heroin and cocaine to be in the same category as alcohol. C) in a democracy, laws must be based on the fundamental beliefs and values of majority of citizens. D) American popular opinion has always been moulded primarily by the values of the middle class.

choices.A: Using drugs is widely available, they will be abused despite laws to the contrary.
choices.B: most Americans consider heroin and cocaine to be in the same category as alcohol.
choices.C: in a democracy, laws must be based on the fundamental beliefs and values of majority of citizens.
choices.D: American popular opinion has always been moulded primarily by the values of the middle class.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Philosophy should be taught to students at a very early age. It will instil in them a healthy scepticism towards values that they might otherwise accept without question.
